footer.disclaimer: This website is a public advocacy initiative. All personal information has been anonymized. WELLCHIP is a publicly listed company (Bursa: 5325).

home.heroSubtitle: A Bursa-listed pawnshop chain charged customers above the legal interest rate — and above their own ticket rates. We documented it. Now we're helping others.
home.caseTitle: The Case That Started It All
home.caseSummary: In December 2025, Mr. C pawned a gold bracelet at a WELLCHIP (大元當) branch in Johor Bahru for RM 4,000. The pawn ticket stated interest at 1% first month, 1.5% subsequent months — meaning RM 40.50/month. Instead, the shop charged RM 100/month — a rate of 2.5%, exceeding both the ticket rate and the legal maximum of 2% under the Pawnbrokers Act 1972.
home.caseAdmission: When confronted, the shop stated they "no longer follow the receipt given" — a direct admission of guilt.
home.casePoliceReport: Police report LARKIN/006020/26 was filed on 1 May 2026.
home.why1Desc: WELLCHIP (stock code 5325) is publicly listed on Bursa Malaysia with 27+ outlets. This is not a small-shop issue — it's systemic.
home.why2Desc: The Pawnbrokers Act 1972 caps interest at 2% per month. WELLCHIP charged 2.5% — a criminal offence.
home.why3Desc: If this happened to you at any pawnshop in Malaysia, submit your complaint. Together we can push for enforcement.
home.complaintsDesc: If you've experienced overcharging at any pawnshop in Malaysia, your story matters. Submit a complaint and join others in seeking justice.

story.title: The Full Story
story.subtitle: How a routine gold pawn turned into a documented case of systematic overcharging by a Bursa-listed pawnshop chain.
story.t1Desc: Mr. C pawns a gold bracelet (9.1g, 2 items) at WELLCHIP Larkin branch for RM 4,000. Ticket YC/0695 clearly states: 1% first month, 1.5% subsequent months. Correct monthly payment: RM 40.50.
story.t2Desc: Month 2 payment: shop charges RM 100.50 instead of RM 40.50 — an overcharge of RM 60.00. When questioned, the owner says he "cannot do anything" and tells customer to "just follow the app."
story.t4Desc: RM 40.50 charged correctly via Touch 'n Go.
story.t5Desc: Mr. C goes to redeem the gold bracelet. The shop again charges RM 100 — an overcharge of RM 59.50. The shop explicitly states: "We no longer follow the receipt given." A witness was present.
story.t6Desc: Police report LARKIN/006020/26 filed at Balai Polis Larkin. The report documents the systematic overcharging and the shop's admission.
story.evidenceSubtitle: Key documents from the case. Personal information has been redacted.
story.ev3Desc: App screenshot showing RM 100.50 overcharge on 6 Feb 2026
story.ev4Desc: App showing RM 100 interest payable — above both ticket and legal rates
story.ev5Desc: WELLCHIP pawn ticket clearly showing 1%/1.5% interest rate and RM 4,000 loan
story.v1: Charged rate (2.5%) exceeds the legal maximum of 2% under Section 17, Pawnbrokers Act 1972
story.v2: Charged rate exceeds the rate printed on their own pawn ticket (1%/1.5%)
story.v3: Shop's admission — "no longer follow the receipt" — constitutes deliberate breach
story.v4: Potential criminal offence under Section 420, Penal Code (cheating)

rights.title: Know Your Rights
rights.subtitle: Understanding the Pawnbrokers Act 1972 and how it protects you.
rights.interestDesc: Under the Pawnbrokers Act 1972 (Section 17) and the 2004 Regulations, the maximum interest rate a pawnbroker can charge is:
rights.interestNote: If your pawn ticket states a lower rate (e.g., 1% or 1.5%), the pawnbroker MUST charge that lower rate. Charging above the ticket rate is both a breach of contract and a statutory offence.
rights.r1: Right to a pawn ticket stating loan amount, interest rate, and redemption terms
rights.r2: Right to extension of at least 3 months if unable to redeem by deadline
rights.r3: Right to accurate weighing of jewellery items
rights.r4: Right to surplus proceeds if unredeemed item is auctioned (notification within 7 days)
rights.r5: Right to inspect records within 4 months of auction
rights.r6: Right to compensation at loan amount + 25% if pledged items are lost/damaged
rights.r7: Right to a free copy of records if pawn ticket is lost
rights.channel1Desc: Visit your nearest police station. Bring: pawn ticket, payment receipts, IC, photos of evidence, written timeline. For fraud cases, in-person reporting is recommended.
rights.channel2Desc: KPKT is the licensing authority for pawnbrokers. File via SISPAA online portal (kpkt.spab.gov.my), by email (bkkk@kpkt.gov.my), or call 03-8000 8000. KPKT can suspend/revoke licenses and order restitution.
rights.channel3Desc: If the pawnshop ignores your demand letter, file a claim with the Consumer Tribunal (only RM 5 fee). The tribunal's decision is legally binding.
rights.channel4Desc: Call 03-7877 9000 or visit ncccaduan.firebaseapp.com. NCCC can mediate and escalate to KPKT or PDRM.

complaints.title: Public Complaints
complaints.subtitle: Stories from pawnshop customers across Malaysia. All complaints are verified before publishing.
complaints.noComplaints: No verified complaints yet. Be the first to submit yours.
complaints.submitSubtitle: If you've been overcharged by a pawnshop in Malaysia, tell us your story. All complaints are reviewed before being published publicly.
complaints.formDescriptionHint: Include details: what was the agreed rate, what were you charged, what did the shop say when you questioned them?
complaints.formEvidenceHint: Photos of pawn tickets, receipts, app screenshots. Max 5 MB each.
complaints.formSuccess: Your complaint has been submitted successfully. It will be reviewed and published once verified.
complaints.formError: Something went wrong. Please try again.
complaints.formPrivacy: Your phone number and email are kept private. Only your name, pawnshop details, and description are shown publicly after review.

about.title: About PawnJustice
about.subtitle: A public advocacy initiative to protect pawnshop customers in Malaysia.
about.whoDesc: PawnJustice was started by ordinary Malaysians who discovered that a Bursa-listed pawnshop chain was systematically overcharging customers — charging interest rates above both their own ticket rates and the legal maximum under the Pawnbrokers Act 1972.
about.mission1: Document and expose pawnshop overcharging practices
about.mission2: Educate the public about their rights under the Pawnbrokers Act 1972
about.mission3: Collect and publicize victim complaints to create regulatory pressure
about.mission4: Push for stronger KPKT enforcement against licensed pawnbrokers
about.transparencyDesc: All case details on this site are based on real events. Names have been anonymized to protect privacy. WELLCHIP is named because it is a publicly listed company (Bursa: 5325) and the overcharging is a matter of public interest. Evidence photos have personal information redacted.
about.contactDesc: For media inquiries, legal support offers, or questions about this site, please submit a complaint through our form or reach out via the channels listed on the Your Rights page.
